title: Step 2 — Spec: skills-thin-down (16 → ~10, orchestration-only) + panel gate as enforced contract
description: Create + drive spec via 'mindspec spec create skills-thin-down'. Addresses Max's three concerns: (a) overlap with existing skills, (b) not fully thought through with full mindspec context, (c) too many. SCOPE from the 2026-06-10 skills audit: 1. DELETE /ms-spec-status (pure passthrough of mindspec instruct; internal/setup/claude.go ~line 593). 2. MOVE operational knowledge from skill prose into CLI point-of-use output: MINDSPEC_ALLOW_MAIN escape-hatch hint into the commit-gate error (currently duplicated in ms-bead-fix:74-81 + ms-spec-final-review:96-106); ADR-divergence repair path into the complete error (currently ms-bead-merge); worktree-fallback recovery into mindspec next itself (verbatim copies in ms-bead-next:39-41 + ms-bead-impl:28-33). 3. CONSOLIDATE triplicated guardrails prose (no-complete/no-push/no-scope-creep across ms-bead-prep/impl/fix) into one AGENTS.md section skills reference. 4. EXTRACT artifact-gate HARD-block decision logic (ms-panel-tally:34-40 vs ms-spec-final-review:59-74, diverging) to one authoritative doc. 5. ADD PreToolUse hook enforcing >=5/6 panel APPROVE before mindspec complete — the plugin's central convention becomes an enforced contract no skill can bypass (FINDINGS Part 2, High). 6. ADD mindspec instruct --panel-state for compaction recovery (FINDINGS item 8). 7. Adjudicate remaining FINDINGS items 1 (parallel-window YAGNI), 2 (/ms-panel-halt-recover), 9, 10 (README rationale). Closes out most of mindspec-ch8h.
acceptance_criteria: Skill count reduced ~16→~10 with zero operational-knowledge loss (moved to CLI point-of-use output); /ms-spec-status deleted; escape-hatch/ADR-repair/worktree-fallback emitted by CLI errors; guardrails prose single-sourced; PreToolUse panel-gate hook enforces >=5/6 APPROVE before mindspec complete; instruct --panel-state ships; mindspec-ch8h closed.

title: Step 1 — Spec: agent-contract-hardening (field-note fixes + LLM-harness regression scenarios)
description: Create + drive spec via 'mindspec spec create agent-contract-hardening'. PRINCIPLE: every guard failure emits its recovery command; every terminal command leaves the caller in a valid state; exit codes never lie. SCOPE: the 5 field-note beads — mindspec-3smk (phase-mismatch deadlock, P1), mindspec-qxsy (deleted-cwd getcwd/exit-1, P1), mindspec-i4ad (complete clean-tree not artifact-aware), mindspec-tjat (wrong-directory guard errors), mindspec-v7ez (approval-gate discoverability) — plus same-class backlog beads to triage in: mindspec-s2k3 (approve-impl merge conflicts), mindspec-pi24 (premature merge-main), mindspec-e6qq (over-strict plan validation), mindspec-ku9d (agents skip lifecycle commands), and FINDINGS root causes worth pulling upstream: plan-dep → bd dep sync at plan approve (FINDINGS item 7), .gitignore/root-dotfile OWNERSHIP default (item 6), Dolt 1105 large-description fallback (item 5). NOVEL REQUIREMENT: each fix is pinned by an LLM-harness regression scenario (internal/harness, llm-test skills) replaying the original field-note failure — agent UX becomes a tested product surface. Field notes source: the 2026-06-10 Claude Code session summary recorded in beads mindspec-3smk..v7ez descriptions.
acceptance_criteria: Spec created+approved+implemented: every targeted guard failure emits its recovery command; impl approve/complete leave caller in valid state with honest exit codes; each field-note failure (3smk qxsy i4ad tjat v7ez) has an LLM-harness regression scenario; triaged beads (pi24 e6qq ku9d 2c80) folded in or explicitly deferred.

title: Usability roadmap 2026-06: agent-contract hardening + skills thin-down + 091 + backlog burn-down
description:
Master plan agreed with Max on 2026-06-10, synthesizing: (1) real-user field notes from a Claude Code session driving mindspec (5 beads: mindspec-3smk, qxsy, i4ad, tjat, v7ez), (2) skills-overlap audit of the 16 shipped skills (11 plugin + 5 lifecycle), (3) the open-beads backlog, (4) in-flight work: PR #118 (plugin spec-050 followups) and spec 091 (ownership-discovery, drafted, awaiting user-gated spec approve — handover at /Users/Max/replit/SPEC-091-HANDOVER.md).

UNIFYING DIAGNOSIS: the agent contract lives in prose (16 skills, instruct templates, docs) while the binary's errors are dead-ends (no recovery path, dishonest exit codes, location/artifact-blind guards). Skills grew large BECAUSE they compensate for what the CLI doesn't say at point of use. Fix the CLI's contract and the skills shrink naturally.

NORTH-STAR METRIC: an agent completes a spec end-to-end with zero manual rescues, measured via LLM-harness scenarios (internal/harness + llm-test skills) that encode each field-note failure as a regression test.

SEQUENCE: Step 0 hygiene → Step 1 agent-contract-hardening spec → Step 2 skills-thin-down spec → Step 3 spec 091 approve+implement (first real-world exercise of the hardened lifecycle; re-audit its line-number citations first, 3 weeks of drift) → Step 4 validator-bug batch. Steps are the child beads of this epic.
design: Why this order: Step 1 fixes what breaks every agent session today, enables most of Step 2's deletions (operational knowledge moves from skill prose into CLI errors), and de-risks Step 3 (091 implementation runs complete/impl-approve across many beads). Steps 1 and 2 are each a normal mindspec spec (~6-8 beads). Skills audit verdict: no hard conflicts; one pure-passthrough skill (/ms-spec-status) deletable; duplicated prose that will drift (MINDSPEC_ALLOW_MAIN escape hatch x2, guardrails x3, artifact-gate logic x2, worktree fallback x2).
acceptance_criteria: All five step children closed; agent-contract-hardening and skills-thin-down specs merged to main; spec 091 implemented; LLM-harness scenarios pin every field-note failure; bd backlog reflects reality (no shipped-but-open beads).

title: impl approve / complete delete the worktree the caller is standing in — getcwd errors and spurious exit 1 in agent harnesses
description: Both terminal lifecycle commands remove the worktree the agent is typically standing in. impl approve auto-chdirs INTO the spec worktree (cmd/mindspec/impl.go:54-57) then FinalizeEpic removes it; withWorkingDir (internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go:513-528) silently fails to restore cwd into the deleted dir. Worse, the invoking shell's cwd is left pointing at a deleted directory, so agent harnesses (Claude Code Bash tool) see 'pwd: error retrieving current directory: getcwd: cannot access parent directories' and exit code 1 — AFTER the command fully succeeded. Agents then treat the approve/complete as failed and retry/repair. Compounding: mindspec next's own completion guidance (cmd/mindspec/next.go:272) instructs agents to 'cd into the worktree, then run mindspec complete', i.e. mindspec tells the agent to stand in the directory that complete will delete. Fix: (1) os.Chdir(root) immediately after the terminal mutation, before emitInstruct; (2) when invoked from inside a worktree that the command will remove, print a prominent final line 'NOTE: your shell's directory was removed — run: cd <root>'; (3) change next.go:272 guidance to say complete may be run from the repo root (it is location-agnostic — it resolves the bead worktree from the worktree list). Reported via Claude Code agent field notes 2026-06-10.
acceptance_criteria: Running impl approve / complete from inside the to-be-removed worktree leaves the mindspec process exiting 0, emits a cd-back instruction, and next's completion guidance no longer tells agents to run complete from the bead worktree

title: impl approve: phase-mismatch deadlock when stored mindspec_phase is stale — no recovery path
description: When the epic's stored mindspec_phase metadata disagrees with the child-derived phase (e.g. last bead closed via bd close or a crashed complete, leaving stored=implement while all children are closed), 'mindspec impl approve' fails with: expected review mode, got "implement" (internal/approve/impl.go:121-123). DerivePhaseWithStatusWithCache (internal/phase/derive.go:120-130) trusts the stored phase and only warns on stderr, so the lifecycle deadlocks. The only fix is undocumented manual metadata surgery: bd update <epic> --metadata='{"mindspec_phase":"review"}'. Fix options: (a) when stored phase disagrees with derived phase AND derived phase satisfies the gate, self-heal the metadata; (b) emit the exact recovery command in the error message; (c) add --reconcile-phase flag. Reported via Claude Code agent field notes 2026-06-10.
acceptance_criteria: Running impl approve with stored=implement but all children closed either succeeds (self-heal) or fails with an error that contains the exact recovery command

title: plugins/mindspec: gaps + workflows research findings
description:
Captured live during lola spec-050 session (Beads 2 r2 fix, 3 r1, 4 r1) that wrote and exercised the plugin. Full findings at plugins/mindspec/FINDINGS.md.

Top items (10 follow-ups):

1. README naming — name the 4 Anthropic patterns the plugin implements (parallelization, orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer, prompt chaining) + flag routing as the unused one.
2. /ms-bead-prep — sibling to /ms-bead-impl for drafting impl prompts. Biggest lever on impl-subagent quality, currently undocumented.
3. Codex-substitution as deterministic check — grep for 'ERROR: You've hit your usage limit' rather than prose 'detect failure'.
4. PreToolUse hook on mindspec complete — enforce >=5/6 APPROVE declaratively, not as orchestrator convention.
5. bd Dolt 1105 — large bead descriptions block --claim event-recording. Either resize events.old_value column or have plugin auto-fall-back.
6. .gitignore ADR-divergence — bead merges consistently hit [adr-divergence-unowned] on .gitignore. Either claim under infra domain, treat root dotfiles as repo-infra-by-default, or pre-include --override-adr.
7. bd ready vs plan dep graph drift — Bead 5 showed ready before Bead 4 merged. mindspec plan approve should walk plan **Depends on:** lines and bd dep add automatically.
8. mindspec instruct (SessionStart hook) extension — add --panel-state surfacing in-progress beads, pending panel rounds, locked agent worktrees. Post-compaction state recovery was slow without this.
9. parallel-window in /ms-spec-autopilot — currently prose, not algorithm. Either remove as YAGNI or add a worked example.
10. /ms-panel-halt-recover — resume path after halt/REJECT undefined.

Workflows research (Claude Code Dynamic Workflows, research preview May 2026):
- Real feature: JS runtime, 1000/run, 16 concurrent subagents
- Invoked via /deep-research, ultracode, custom commands
- Distinct from skills/hooks/subagents
- Docs: code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows.md
- Recommendation: stay with skills for panel-review (need mid-run intervention to swap codex-subs); future port for /ms-spec-autopilot only (background-durable across context compaction)

Items 1, 2, 5-10 remain OPEN — parallel-window algorithm, halt recovery, Dolt 1105 auto-handling, .gitignore ADR-divergence, bd-vs-plan dep sync, sessionstart panel-state, 6-vs-other reviewer count rationale, round-2 panel rationale, PreToolUse hook on mindspec complete, README pattern naming.

title: [ZFC-2] Remove "Decomposition Heuristics" block from plan.md template
description:
`internal/instruct/templates/plan.md:95-109` pre-bakes a six-step research-cited rubric into the model's decomposition reasoning (target 3-5 beads, R≈0.50 redundancy, chain depth ≤ 3, etc.). This is exactly the "hand-rolled prompt scaffolding that pre-bakes decision categories" anti-pattern from ZFC: instead of letting the model judge decomposition, the prompt hands it a fixed script with numeric cutoffs.
Replace with a short open-ended directive: "Decompose the spec into the fewest, most-independent beads you can. Justify each split. Prefer fewer larger beads over many small ones; prefer parallel over serial chains." Let the model size the work.
Note: this bead and ZFC-1 are companion fixes — both encode the same opinion, just in different places.
close_reason: Re-review: not actually a ZFC violation. ZFC governs application code, not prompt content. Telling the model 'research suggests 3-5 beads, here's why' informs its judgment with priors — the model still decides. A pure-ZFC prompt that withheld this guidance would be worse, not better. Worth periodic 'is this guidance still right?' review, but that's prompt-design, not ZFC.

title: [PERF-1] Add per-CLI phase.Cache to dedupe redundant bd subprocess calls
description:
`mindspec instruct` (run by SessionStart on every agent turn) makes 5-11 redundant `bd` subprocess calls. The call stack threads `FindEpicBySpecID` 3× through `ResolveTarget` (`resolve/target.go:87`) + `ResolveMode` (`resolve/resolve.go:44`) + `ResolveContextFromDir` (`cmd/mindspec/instruct.go:89`). Inside, `queryEpics` (`phase/derive.go:457-503`) and `queryChildren` (`phase/derive.go:487`) each loop over the three known statuses with one `bd list --status=… --json` per status.
On macOS, fork+exec of a Go binary is ~3-8ms; cold Dolt is far worse — each `bd` call is roughly 30-100ms. Going from 8 calls to 2 saves ~200-600ms per `mindspec instruct`, a 2-5× SessionStart speedup.
design:
1. Introduce `internal/phase/cache.go` with a `Cache` struct memoizing `epicID→EpicInfo`, `epicID→[]ChildInfo`, `specID→epicID`, and a `[]EpicInfo` for all-epics.
2. Make `phase.ResolveContext`, `DerivePhase`, `FindEpicBySpecID`, `resolve.ResolveMode`, `resolve.ResolveTarget`, `state.checkBeadStatus` accept the cache.
3. In `cmd/mindspec/instruct.go:68-105`, build the cache once and thread through; eliminate the duplicate `ResolveContextFromDir` call.
4. Replace the per-status loops in `queryEpics`/`queryChildren` with a single `bd list --json` (no `--status` filter) and filter in-process.
acceptance_criteria:
- `mindspec instruct` makes ≤3 bd subprocess calls in the warm case (verified via `mindspec trace`).
- All `phase`/`resolve` unit tests pass.
- SessionStart wall-clock drops measurably (compare `time mindspec instruct` before/after).
close_reason: Shipped via PR #109 (internal/phase/cache.go); verified 2026-06-10

title: [PERF-2] Replace SessionStart subprocess spawn with in-process instruct call
description: `cmd/mindspec/hook.go:126-138` does `exec.CommandContext(ctx, os.Args[0], "instruct").Run()`. The hook is already running inside the mindspec binary; spawning itself doubles cobra init, doubles trace setup, doubles ADR/config loading. There is no reason to fork — the same `instructCmd.RunE` body can be invoked in-process.
design:
1. Extract the shared body from `instructCmd.RunE` into `internal/instruct.Run(format, specFlag string) error`.
2. Replace the subprocess call in `runSessionStartHook` with `instruct.Run("", "")`.
3. Remove the 12s context timeout (errors should surface directly).
close_reason: Shipped via PR #94 commit a1e4443; hook.go:131 calls instruct.Run in-process; verified 2026-06-10

title: [SEC-1] Validate spec/ADR/bead IDs at all CLI entrypoints before filesystem joins
description:
User-supplied IDs reach `filepath.Join` + `os.ReadFile`/`os.WriteFile` without `validate.SpecID` at many call sites. Only 4 sites currently validate.
Concrete locations:
- `cmd/mindspec/validate.go:31` — `validate.ValidateSpec(root, specID)` (no SpecID check)
- `cmd/mindspec/validate.go:50` — same for `ValidatePlan`
- `cmd/mindspec/spec.go:96` — `approve.ApproveSpec(root, specID, …)`
- `internal/approve/plan.go:71-78` — same pattern
- `cmd/mindspec/adr.go:104-119` — `store.Get(args[0])` → `filepath.Join(dir, id+".md")` and `filepath.Glob(...)`
- `internal/adr/create.go:29` — `--supersedes` flag value joined with ADRDir; `Supersede(root, oldID, newID)` does `ReadFile`/`WriteFile` on the joined path. `--supersedes ../../../tmp/poisoned` writes an ADR template to `/tmp/poisoned.md`.
- `cmd/mindspec/domain.go:32` (via `workspace.DomainDir`)
- `cmd/mindspec/record.go:48,70` (via `recording.HasRecording`/`RecordingDir`)
- `cmd/mindspec/spec.go:41` (via `workspace.SpecDir`)
- `internal/recording/manifest.go:33-44` — `RecordingDir`/`ManifestPath`/`EventsPath` don't validate
The bite is bounded because mindspec runs as the local user, but scripts/CI that pass IDs derived from branch names, tickets, or external strings get a "read/write arbitrary `.md`-named file" primitive.
design:
Two-layer fix:
1. Add `validate.BeadID`, `validate.ADRID`, `validate.DomainName` helpers next to existing `validate.SpecID`.
2. Make every workspace path-builder return `(string, error)` so the type system forces the check — `workspace.SpecDir(root, specID string) (string, error)`, similar for `ADRDir(root, adrID)`, `DomainDir(root, domain)`, `recording.*Path`.
3. After path construction, optionally also pass through `validate.SafePath(root, resolved)` (already exists) as defense-in-depth.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #108; helpers live in internal/idvalidate (not internal/validate); verified 2026-06-10

title: Spec epics left open when merged via PR without mindspec impl approve
description:
Specs 068, 071, 072 were merged to main via GitHub PRs but their lifecycle epics remain open with zero children.

Design intent:
- Epics auto-close via beads molecule completion when all child beads close
- impl approve then sets the mindspec_done metadata marker to distinguish 'all beads done' from 'explicitly finalized'

Why it failed for these specs:
- plan approve didn't create child beads under the epic (likely due to SpecIDFromMetadata slug bug — mindspec-xd3g — preventing epic lookup)
- With zero children, molecule completion can't fire
- impl approve was never run (and would also fail due to the slug bug)

Root cause: mindspec-xd3g (SpecIDFromMetadata doesn't slugify). Once that's fixed, plan approve will create beads under the correct epic, molecule completion will auto-close the epic when all beads close, and impl approve will set the done marker.

Immediate cleanup: close these three orphaned epics with done marker manually.
close_reason: Root cause (mindspec-xd3g slugify) is fixed. All 3 orphaned epics (068, 071, 072) are already closed.

title: Agent can bypass bead worktree workflow: instruct doesn't guide to mindspec next
description:
When starting a session with an active spec in implement phase, the agent is not told to use 'mindspec next' to claim beads and create bead worktrees. This allows the agent to commit implementation code directly to the spec branch.

Three contributing factors:

1. **instruct from main shows 'No Active Work'**: Even with spec 073 active (branch + worktree + beads), running instruct from the main repo shows idle. It should detect the active spec branch/worktree and tell the agent to cd to the worktree and use 'mindspec next'.

2. **instruct from spec worktree shows stale test epics**: The phase resolver finds hundreds of stale test epics from LLM test sandbox runs, flooding the output. The real spec 073 is buried.

3. **Pre-commit hook only protects main**: The hook checks cfg.IsProtectedBranch() which only matches main/master. It does NOT enforce that implementation code belongs on bead/ branches instead of spec/ branches. Even if core.hooksPath weren't bypassed, the hook would have allowed the commits.

The primary fix should be in instruction — the agent should be told what to do. Hooks are a last resort.
notes: Root cause is mindspec-xd3g (SpecIDFromMetadata slug bug). Without the epic match, phase derivation fails and instruct gives wrong guidance. The hook bypass (core.hooksPath) and missing spec-branch enforcement are secondary — hooks should be a last resort. Fix mindspec-xd3g first, then DiscoverActiveSpecs should filter done-phase specs.
close_reason: All three factors fixed: (1) SpecIDFromMetadata slugify (ce44c21), (2) DiscoverActiveSpecs filters done/childless epics (cc2a287), (3) pre-commit hook blocks impl commits on spec/ branches (1088d19).

title: SpecIDFromMetadata doesn't reconstruct slug format — plan approve can't find epic
description:
SpecIDFromMetadata(73, "Llm Test Coverage") returns "073-Llm Test Coverage" but the actual spec ID is "073-llm-test-coverage". The function does fmt.Sprintf("%03d-%s", specNum, specTitle) without lowercasing or hyphenating.

Impact chain:
1. FindEpicBySpecID fails — can't match epic to spec ID from worktree context
2. Phase derivation fails — spec worktree context falls back to default mode
3. instruct gives wrong guidance — "No Active Work" or "Multiple Active Specs" instead of implement mode instructions
4. Agent bypasses workflow — works directly on spec branch without mindspec next / bead worktrees

Also contributes to DiscoverActiveSpecs pollution: stale test epics generate specIDs like "001-Test Feature" (unslugified) and show as active specs.

Fix: SpecIDFromMetadata should slugify the title (lowercase, spaces→hyphens) to match the original slug format.
close_reason: slugify() added to SpecIDFromMetadata, DiscoverActiveSpecs filters done/orphan epics

title: skip_next analyzer false positives on CLI-internal commits
description: The skip_next analyzer rule flags any git commit before mindspec next as a violation. But mindspec CLI commands (spec create, approve spec, approve plan) internally commit as part of their operation (e.g. 'chore: approve spec 001-calc'). These are legitimate lifecycle commits, not agent code edits. The rule needs to distinguish CLI-internal commits from agent-initiated code commits. This caused 13/15 LLM test failures as false positives.

description: After Steps 1-3: (a) validator bug cluster as one small batch or fix branch: mindspec-53qx (new-ADR chicken-and-egg), mindspec-ew79 (adr-cite-missing should look at spec branch), mindspec-f115 (status parser vs parenthetical extensions), mindspec-wgcw (Domain(s) tokenizer splits inside parens). (b) mindspec-wuwi (PERF-2 in-process instruct call, the genuinely-open m557 P1). (c) Re-triage remaining m557 P2/P3s and the rest of the open backlog; defer or close what no longer matters.
acceptance_criteria: Validator cluster (53qx ew79 f115 wgcw) fixed; mindspec-4pkq (PERF-3, still genuinely open) done or deferred with reason; remaining m557 P3s re-triaged.

title: Step 3 — Spec 091 ownership-discovery: approve + implement on the hardened lifecycle
description: USER-GATED: Max runs 'mindspec spec approve 091-ownership-discovery' from .worktrees/worktree-spec-091-ownership-discovery (uncommitted spec.md edits there are auto-committed by approve). Deliberately sequenced after Step 1 so 091's multi-bead implementation is the first real-world exercise of the hardened complete/impl-approve lifecycle. BEFORE implementation: re-audit the spec's line-number citations (e.g. ownership.go:46-52) — drafted 2026-05-22, three+ weeks of main drift. First plan bead must do the buildMigratePrompt insertion-site audit + create ADR-0035 (handover §6). Full handover: /Users/Max/replit/SPEC-091-HANDOVER.md.
acceptance_criteria: Spec 091 approved by Max, implemented per its 23 ACs on the hardened lifecycle; line-number citations re-audited before first bead; ADR-0035 created by first plan bead.

title: Targeted wrong-directory errors: use DetectWorktreeContext to name the expected location on guard failures
description: Agents repeatedly run lifecycle commands from the main worktree and hit guards whose errors don't mention location. The commands are largely location-agnostic now (Spec 079: complete resolves the bead worktree from the worktree list; impl approve auto-chdirs), but the failure modes that remain are opaque: e.g. next's dirty-tree guard run from main blocks on main's dirt even though the agent's work lives in a worktree, and stale docs still claim 'next must run from the spec worktree'. Fix: (a) when a guard fails, call workspace.DetectWorktreeContext (internal/workspace/workspace.go:250-275) and append 'you are in the <main|spec|bead> worktree; this check applies to <path>' to the error; (b) audit README/plugin skill docs for stale location requirements and align with actual location-agnostic behavior. Reported via Claude Code agent field notes 2026-06-10.
acceptance_criteria: Guard failures in next/complete/impl-approve name the worktree context the command evaluated and the directory the agent should use; docs no longer assert location requirements the code doesn't have

title: complete: clean-tree check is not artifact-aware (ADR-0025) — pre-commit bd export re-dirty blocks completion
description: mindspec next uses the artifact-aware guard next.CheckDirtyTree (internal/next/guard.go:106-127) which classifies .beads/issues.jsonl as co-managed dirt and normalizes it via bd export. mindspec complete does NOT: step 3 of complete.Run calls exec.IsTreeClean(checkPath) (internal/complete/complete.go:181, internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go:382-389) which is a plain git-status check. When the project's pre-commit hook re-exports .beads/issues.jsonl during the auto-commit, the tree is dirty again immediately after committing, and complete fails with 'workspace has uncommitted changes'. Field-reported workaround was git commit --no-verify or git config core.hooksPath /dev/null — both bad. Fix: route complete's clean check through the same ADR-0025 classification (artifact dirt → bd export + re-check or fold into the auto-commit; only user dirt blocks). Reported via Claude Code agent field notes 2026-06-10.
acceptance_criteria: complete succeeds when the only dirt is .beads/issues.jsonl; user-authored dirt still blocks with the existing hint

title: create-from-plan: inlining cited ADRs into every bead description overflows dolt TEXT column (~65535B)
description:
mindspec bead create-from-plan embeds the full text of every ADR listed in
the plan's adr_citations frontmatter into each bead's description. For a
plan that cites 6-8 ADRs of any meaningful size, the resulting description
exceeds bd's underlying dolt TEXT column limit (~65,535 bytes) and bd
silently fails with `Error 1105: string '...' is too long for column
'description'`.

Repro on lola spec-044-whatsapp-integration:
- 8 ADRs cited in adr_citations frontmatter (including the
  ~10KB ADR-0030, ADR-0029, ADR-0033, etc).
- mindspec bead create-from-plan 044-whatsapp-integration
- First bead create call constructs a ~90KB --description argument and
  bd rejects: 'Error 1105: string ... is too long for column description'.

Behaviour confirmed by direct bd test:
  - 60KB body via --body-file → succeeds.
  - 80KB body via --body-file → fails with Error 1105.

Workaround options:
- A) Drop some ADR citations from the plan frontmatter (re-breaks
     adr-coverage-missing for affected domains).
- B) Author beads manually via `bd create --body-file` with just the
     bead body (no ADR inlining), then set --metadata to match
     mindspec's expected schema.

Fix proposals:
- Don't inline cited ADRs into bead descriptions — cite by ID
  (e.g. "see ADR-0029") and let the bead reader cross-reference.
- Or: inline at most a 1-2-line summary line per cited ADR.
- Or: route bead description through --body-file (file-on-disk) so
  the CLI-arg path doesn't matter, AND switch the dolt column to
  MEDIUMTEXT (~16MB) so larger descriptions actually fit.

Reverse triage: every spec with >5 ADR citations and non-trivial ADR
sizes will hit this. lola spec-044 is the first plan we've authored
that explicitly cites 8 ADRs; earlier specs cited 1-3.

title: validator: new-ADR-with-spec chicken-and-egg — Proposed ADRs can't satisfy adr-coverage
description:
The plan validator's adr-coverage-missing check requires that every Impacted
Domain in the spec be covered by at least one *Accepted* ADR cited in the
plan's adr_citations frontmatter.

But the natural workflow for an ADR introduced *by* a spec is:

1. Spec drafted; identifies a new architectural pattern.
2. ADR drafted alongside as 'Proposed' (per ADR-0030 precedent of landing
   ADRs to main at spec time, not at impl time).
3. Spec/plan approved; impl proceeds.
4. After impl ships and the pattern is validated in production, the ADR
   flips Proposed → Accepted.

But adr-coverage-missing blocks step 3 because the ADR is still Proposed.
Either the ADR has to be flipped to Accepted prematurely (lying about
production-validation status), or its domain coverage has to be papered
over by an already-Accepted ADR that doesn't really cover the new
behaviour. Both are wrong.

Discovered during lola spec-044 plan-approve: ADR-0033 was the new ADR
introduced by spec-044, status 'Proposed', so api/app/merge_claims/
domain failed coverage. Workaround used: flip ADR-0033 to Accepted early.

Fix proposal: extend adr-coverage to accept Proposed-status ADRs *if* the
ADR is explicitly cited in the plan's adr_citations (the citation is the
acknowledgement of intent). Or add a per-spec opt-in: 'this ADR is part of
the spec, expect Proposed during impl, accept after merge'.

title: validator: Domain(s) tokenizer splits on commas inside parens, breaking compound strings
description:
The ADR-frontmatter (or in-prose) 'Domain(s):' line is tokenized by splitting
on comma globally. This breaks strings like:

    Domain(s): core, webapp (\`app/\`, react navigation native-stack), api/app/...

which the spec author meant as three items (core; the webapp paren-block;
the api/app path), but the validator interprets as four items by splitting
inside the parenthesised qualifier — yielding a domain literally named
'react navigation native-stack)' that nothing on the spec side can match.

Discovered during lola spec-044 plan-approve trying to match the spec's
Impacted Domains tag 'webapp (\`app/\`, react navigation native-stack)' against
ADR-0007's Domain(s) tag. The validator's split changed the right-hand value
mid-flight and the match failed.

Fix proposal: parser should be paren-aware — only split on commas at the
top level (depth 0), not commas inside () or [] or {}.

Workaround used: rewrite Domain(s) to avoid internal commas (e.g. use 'and'
or semicolons, or simplify to a single short string).

title: validator: adr-coverage status parser doesn't handle parenthetical extensions like 'Accepted (...)'
description:
ADR Status frontmatter line of the form:

    Status: Accepted (3 panel rounds + final tightening; converged 4/4)

is not recognised by the plan validator's adr-coverage check. The bare value
'Accepted' is treated as Accepted, but anything-after-Accepted (parenthetical
qualifier with provenance/notes) is treated as not-Accepted, causing
adr-coverage-missing to fire for any domain covered only by that ADR.

Discovered during lola spec-044 plan-approve: ADR-0029 had status 'Accepted
(3 panel rounds + final tightening; converged 4/4)' and the validator
reported its api/app/inbound/whatsapp/ and infra/tofu/ domains as having no
cited Accepted ADR even though ADR-0029 was clearly Accepted.

Workaround used: strip the parenthetical, leaving plain 'Accepted'.

Fix proposal: parse Status as the first token on the line (or up to the first
whitespace / paren), not the entire line. Allow free-form qualifier after.

title: validator: adr-cite-missing should also look at ADRs on the spec branch, not just main
description:
## Problem

\`mindspec plan approve <spec-id>\` runs the plan validator's \`adr-cite-missing\` check by looking for cited ADRs in the canonical registry (i.e., the version on main). This fails for any spec that introduces a NEW ADR as part of its own work.

Example (lola repo, spec 044):
- Spec 044 introduces ADR-0033 (cross-channel identity + session merge protocol). The ADR file lives at \`.mindspec/docs/adr/ADR-0033-...\` on \`spec/044-whatsapp-integration\`.
- Running \`mindspec plan approve 044-whatsapp-integration\` from that branch produces: \`adr-cite-missing: cited ADR ADR-0033-cross-channel-identity-and-session-merge does not exist\`
- The validator can't see ADR-0033 because it's only on the feature branch.

## Why this matters

This blocks the canonical mindspec flow for any spec that introduces a new ADR:
- Spec is approved, plan is drafted, plan cites a new ADR landing with the spec → validation fails.
- The only workarounds today are: (a) revise existing ADRs to absorb the new domain (treats accepted ADRs as mutable), or (b) round-trip the new ADR to main via a small docs-only PR before plan-approve.

(b) is what just happened in lola PR #524: ADR-0033 had to be PR'd to main as a standalone docs commit so \`mindspec plan approve 044-whatsapp-integration\` could see it. ADR-0030 precedent says new ADRs land at spec time, but the validator's main-only view means they have to land BEFORE plan-approve, which is awkward sequencing.

## Proposed fix

The plan validator's \`adr-cite-missing\` check should:
1. First check the canonical registry (current main, or whatever the validator's default base is).
2. Then check the current spec branch (i.e., HEAD of the worktree the validator is running in).
3. Only fail if the ADR is missing from BOTH.

This matches the realistic lifecycle: an ADR introduced by spec N can be cited by spec N's plan because it WILL be on main once spec N ships, but it's already on the spec branch and visible to the worktree right now.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Plan validator can cite an ADR that exists only on the current spec branch (i.e., committed in the worktree where validation runs) without failing \`adr-cite-missing\`.
- [ ] Existing main-only check still works for ADRs not introduced by this spec.
- [ ] Test case: a spec branch that introduces a new ADR file + cites it in plan.md should pass \`mindspec plan approve\` even when the ADR is not yet on main.
- [ ] Documentation update: the mindspec ADR-0030 precedent ("load-bearing ADRs land at spec time") is internally consistent with the validator.

## Out of scope

- The other related issue (\`adr-cite-irrelevant\` / \`adr-coverage-missing\` complaining about domain string mismatch between spec Impacted Domains file-path-style strings and ADR \`Domain(s):\` vocabulary strings) is a separate concern about validator strictness, not visibility.

## Context

- Lola PR #524: https://github.com/nologohq/lola/pull/524 — the workaround docs-only ADR-0033 landing
- Lola spec 044 plan-approve attempt: blocked on this validator behavior

title: mindspec spec create should interrogate the author for spec details (--interactive grill mode)
description:
**Feature request:** `mindspec spec create` currently scaffolds an empty markdown template
with placeholder sections (`<Brief description...>`, `<domain-1>`, etc.) that the author
then fills in by hand. The author can write a vague or thin spec and the tool will accept
it — only `mindspec validate spec` later catches structural gaps (open questions,
missing ADRs, sparse acceptance criteria).

**Proposal:** turn spec-create into an interactive interrogation that grills the author
for the load-bearing details up front, producing a spec that already passes structural
validation. Probable prompts:

- Goal (one-paragraph user outcome — refuse empty / one-sentence answers)
- Impacted domains (must enumerate at least one; cross-check against
  `.mindspec/docs/domains/` to suggest matches)
- ADR touchpoints (does this contradict any accepted ADR? does it need a new ADR?
  if yes, suggest the next free number)
- Requirements (must be falsifiable — interactive coach if the author writes "support X"
  without acceptance criteria)
- Hard constraints (HC-1..HC-N from the converged transformation plan — suggest the
  standard set)
- Out-of-scope (refuse empty — every spec has out-of-scope items)
- Acceptance criteria (at least 3, each with a runnable validation proof — coach the
  author if they write soft criteria like "feature works")
- Open questions (if any remain, refuse to mark the spec ready for approval until
  resolved in-tool or explicitly deferred)

**Non-goals:** this is NOT a replacement for human spec authoring. The grill mode
should be opt-in (`mindspec spec create --interactive` or default with `--no-interactive`
escape hatch). Power users who know what they want should still be able to drop into
the template and write freely.

**Why it matters:** specs 085-090 demonstrated that the marginal cost of one more
specific requirement / one more falsifiable AC / one more validation proof is small,
but the marginal value at implementation time is large (every shipped spec caught real
issues at planning or implementation time that would have shipped silently with a
thinner spec). Grilling the author makes that rigor the default, not a discipline
that only experienced authors apply.

**Likely scope:** a new `internal/spec/interview/` package that drives the prompts via
stdin (or a structured TUI via something like `bubbletea` if we want to be fancy), plus
a `--interactive` flag on `spec create`. Schema-driven: each section knows its required
shape, its "refuse if empty" rules, and its coaching hints.

**Related:** spec 091 (this branch) is about OWNERSHIP.yaml discovery gaps that only
surfaced because spec 086 was thorough enough to be reviewable; this bead is about
making that thoroughness the default for every spec going forward.

title: [089-ceremony-collapse] Bead 3 — `mindspec doctor --dry-run-migration` + ADR-0034 finalization
description:
**Title**: `[089] doctor --dry-run-migration reporter + ADR-0034 status finalization`
**Type**: `task`
**Parent epic**: spec 089 lifecycle epic
**Depends on**: Bead 2 (the dry-run reporter shares the
`readStoredPhase` + `DerivePhaseFromChildren` read sequence with
the migrator; Bead 2 establishes the call-site invariants the
reporter parallels)
**Estimated effort**: ~2-3 h

### Goal

Add the `--dry-run-migration` flag to `mindspec doctor`,
implement the reporter that walks `.mindspec/docs/specs/`, and
finalize ADR-0034's `Status` block to remove the stub language.

### Files touched

- **MODIFIED** `cmd/mindspec/doctor.go` — register the new
  `--dry-run-migration` bool flag (parallel to the existing
  `--fix` and `--force` flags at line 75-76); plumb into
  `doctor.Options.DryRunMigration`.
- **MODIFIED** `internal/doctor/doctor.go` — extend the
  `Options` struct with `DryRunMigration bool`; in
  `RunWithOptions`, when set, call the new reporter from
  `internal/doctor/migration.go` and skip the other
  fix-oriented checks.
- **MODIFIED** `internal/doctor/migration.go` — add
  `checkDryRunMigration(r *Report, root string)` that walks
  `.mindspec/docs/specs/`, identifies legacy epics lacking
  `mindspec_phase`, and appends one `Check{Name: "would-migrate:
  spec=<id>", Status: Warn, Message: "epic=<id>
  phase=<derived>"}` per legacy spec. Writes NOTHING.
- **NEW** `internal/doctor/dry_run_migration_test.go` — the four
  reporter tests from the Testing Strategy block.
- **MODIFIED** `.mindspec/docs/adr/ADR-0034-ceremony-collapse.md`
  — replace the `## Status` block paragraph "Stub created during
  spec 089-ceremony-collapse drafting. Finalized in spec 089
  Bead 3 alongside the auto-migrator implementation." (lines
  14-16) with the finalized text naming the merged
  implementation surfaces.

**Steps**

1. **Options struct extension.** In `internal/doctor/doctor.go`,
   add `DryRunMigration bool` to the `Options` struct (around
   line 62) with a comment block explaining: "when true,
   doctor skips its repair checks and runs only the
   `checkDryRunMigration` reporter, which walks all specs and
   reports which would migrate on their next lifecycle command.
   Writes nothing."
2. **Reporter.** In `internal/doctor/migration.go`, add
   `checkDryRunMigration(r *Report, root string)`. The reporter
   uses `filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(root, ".mindspec",
   "docs", "specs", "*"))` to enumerate spec directories,
   parses each directory basename as a spec ID, and for each
   spec ID:
   1. Calls `phase.FindEpicBySpecID(specID)`. Skip on empty.
   2. Resolves the epic via a single `bd show` and reads
      metadata; if `mindspec_phase` is present, skip.
   3. Queries children via `phase.NewCache().GetChildren`,
      computes `phase.DerivePhaseFromChildren(children)`.
   4. Appends `Check{Name: fmt.Sprintf("would-migrate:
      spec=%s", specID), Status: Warn, Message:
      fmt.Sprintf("epic=%s phase=%s", epicID, derived)}` to
      the report.
   Excluded-tree guard runs against each resolved path with
   `strings.HasPrefix` checks for `viz/`, `agentmind/`,
   `bench/` (defensive; specs are not under those trees per
   HC-4 but the guard makes the contract explicit).
3. **Run wiring.** In `RunWithOptions` (line 72), before the
   existing `checkDocs(r, root)` call at line 74, branch on
   `opts.DryRunMigration`: if true, call
   `checkDryRunMigration(r, root)` and return early. The
   dry-run path does not run any other check (per spec
   Requirement 11: "Exit code is 0 if all walks succeed
   regardless of how many legacy specs exist").
4. **CLI flag.** In `cmd/mindspec/doctor.go`, add
   `doctorCmd.Flags().Bool("dry-run-migration", false, "Report
   which specs would migrate on their next lifecycle command
   without writing any state")` (parallel to the existing
   `Bool("fix", …)` registration at line 75). Plumb the value
   into `doctor.Options.DryRunMigration` (parallel to the
   existing `Force: force` plumbing at line 36 — the new line
   reads `dryRun, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run-migration")`
   then `doctor.RunWithOptions(root, doctor.Options{Force:
   force, DryRunMigration: dryRun})`).
5. **ADR-0034 finalization.** In
   `.mindspec/docs/adr/ADR-0034-ceremony-collapse.md`, replace
   the current `## Status` block paragraph (lines 13-16) with:

   ```markdown
   ## Status

   Accepted. Implemented in spec 089-ceremony-collapse:
   - `phase.EnsureMigrated` at `internal/phase/derive.go` (Bead 2)
   - Call sites in `internal/approve/plan.go`,
     `internal/approve/impl.go`, `internal/complete/complete.go`
     (Bead 2)
   - `mindspec doctor --dry-run-migration` reporter in
     `internal/doctor/migration.go` (Bead 3)
   - Regression guard `TestNoMoleculeScaffoldingSymbols` in
     `internal/lint/scaffold_test.go` (Bead 1)
   ```

   No other ADR-0034 section is edited.
6. **Tests.** Create `internal/doctor/dry_run_migration_test.go`
   with the four tests from the Testing Strategy block. Stub
   `phase.FindEpicBySpecID` and the bd children query via the
   existing `phase.SetRunBDForTest` /
   `phase.SetListJSONForTest` seams.

**Verification**

- [ ] `go build ./... && go test -short ./...` is green.
- [ ] `mindspec doctor --dry-run-migration` against a hand-built
  test repo with one legacy + one native spec prints exactly
  one `would-migrate: spec=…` line and exits 0 (smoke test
  noted in commit message; not automated beyond the
  `TestDoctorDryRunMigration*` cases).
- [ ] `go test -v ./...` shows zero new skips or excludes
  relative to `main` (HC-3). Bead 3's commit message records
  the diff.

**Acceptance Criteria**

- [ ] `TestDoctorDryRunMigrationReports` passes; reporter
  walks `.mindspec/docs/specs/`, identifies legacy epics, emits
  a `would-migrate: spec=<id>` Warn check per legacy spec, and
  writes nothing.
- [ ] `TestDoctorDryRunMigrationExitsZeroWithLegacySpecs`
  passes; presence of legacy specs does NOT cause
  `HasFailures()` true (Warn checks only). Per spec
  Requirement 11.
- [ ] `TestDoctorDryRunMigrationSkipsExcludedTrees` passes;
  the walk does not descend into `viz/`, `agentmind/`, or
  `bench/` per HC-4.
- [ ] `TestADR0034FinalizedStatus` passes;
  `ADR-0034-ceremony-collapse.md`'s `## Status` section no
  longer contains "Stub created during spec 089" after Bead 3
  step 5.
- [ ] ADR-0034 finalized at `Status: Accepted` with
  `Domain(s): workflow`; the `## Status` paragraph names the
  merged implementation surfaces (helper, call sites,
  reporter, regression guard).
- [ ] `go build ./... && go test -short ./...` exits 0 per
  HC-5; `go test -v ./...` shows zero new skips/excludes vs
  `main` per HC-3 (diff recorded in commit message).

### Commit message

```
[089] doctor --dry-run-migration reporter + finalize ADR-0034

Adds the --dry-run-migration flag to mindspec doctor. When set, the
doctor walks .mindspec/docs/specs/, identifies legacy 7-bead specs
whose lifecycle epic lacks mindspec_phase metadata, and reports each
as a would-migrate Warn check. Writes nothing.

Also replaces ADR-0034's Status block stub language with the
finalized text naming the implementation surfaces landed in beads 1-3.

Per ADR-0034 decision 3 and spec 089 Requirements 11, 15.
AC: TestDoctorDryRunMigrationReports,
TestDoctorDryRunMigrationExitsZeroWithLegacySpecs,
TestDoctorDryRunMigrationSkipsExcludedTrees,
TestADR0034FinalizedStatus.
```
design:
## Requirements

### Hard Constraints (from converged plan)

1. **HC-1 F5 lands after F1 (087).** F1 (087) and F3 (088) have
   merged; F5 is last in the F4 → F2 → F1 → F3 → F5 chain. No
   direct code dependency between F5 and F1, but the chain
   ordering is the cross-cutting decision per the plan.
2. **HC-2 Solo-developer UX preserved.** Auto-migration runs on
   first lifecycle command with no operator opt-in, no prompt, no
   interactive confirmation. The migration is silent on success
   (a structured log line is emitted to stderr per
   `internal/log` conventions, but no operator action is
   required). `--dry-run-migration` is the only opt-in surface and
   it never writes.
3. **HC-3 Existing test suite preserved.** No test is skipped,
   excluded, or marked `t.Skip` relative to `main`. The existing
   `TestDerivePhaseFromChildren` (`internal/phase/derive_test.go`)
   continues to pass unchanged because `DerivePhaseFromChildren`
   is preserved as the migration's derivation step and as the
   back-stop fallback. The existing `complete_test.go` cases
   continue to pass because the `phase.DerivePhaseFromChildren`
   call at `complete.go:404` is preserved (it now runs against
   post-migration metadata-trusted state, but its inputs and
   outputs are unchanged on the seven-bead test fixtures).
4. **HC-4 `viz/agentmind/bench` excluded.** F5 does not read,
   reference, or include any file under those trees. The doctor
   `--dry-run-migration` walk explicitly skips any spec whose path
   starts with `viz/`, `agentmind/`, or `bench/`. (Specs are under
   `.mindspec/docs/specs/`, none of which is excluded, but the
   walk is defensive.)
5. **HC-5 Every commit `go build ./... && go test -short ./...`
   green.** Each of: (a) the audit/no-op commit (if no
   scaffolding remains, this commit is just a test asserting the
   absence), (b) the `phase.EnsureMigrated` helper introduction
   commit, (c) the three caller-site commits (`approve/plan.go`,
   `approve/impl.go`, `complete/complete.go`), (d) the
   `--dry-run-migration` doctor commit, (e) the ADR-0034 drafting
   commit.
6. **HC-6 AST boundary lint stays green.** The new
   `phase.EnsureMigrated` helper uses only the existing
   `bead.MergeMetadata` and `phase.FindEpicBySpecID` surfaces; it
   adds no new git/process call sites and respects the spec 085
   executor boundary contract. `internal/lint/boundary_test.go`
   stays green.

### Spec-specific (from F5 design)

7. **Audit and remove vestigial molecule-step scaffolding.** Per
   the plan: "grep for `mol.pour` and similar. `closeoutTargets` /
   `EnsureFullyBound` may already be vestigial — confirm and
   delete." Synthesis-time grep confirmed these symbols are
   already absent from the working tree (no matches under
   `--include="*.go"`). This requirement is satisfied by:
   (a) a test `TestNoMoleculeScaffoldingSymbols` that fails if any
   future commit re-introduces the literal identifiers `mol.pour`,
   `closeoutTargets`, or `EnsureFullyBound` anywhere under
   `internal/`; (b) if any such symbol is found at implementation
   time, deletion plus its callers in the same commit.
8. **`phase.EnsureMigrated(specID string) (migrated bool, err
   error)` helper.** New exported function in
   `internal/phase/derive.go`. Algorithm:
   1. Resolve epic via `phase.FindEpicBySpecID(specID)`. If empty
      (no epic exists yet — e.g., `approve spec` is in flight),
      return `(false, nil)` — there is nothing to migrate.
   2. Read epic's stored phase via the existing
      `readStoredPhaseWithCache` helper. If present (non-empty),
      return `(false, nil)` — already migrated (or post-080
      native).
   3. Query children via the existing cache-aware children fetch
      (`queryChildrenWithCache`). Call
      `DerivePhaseFromChildren(children)` to compute the derived
      phase string.
   4. Write the derived phase plus the migration timestamp via a
      single `bead.MergeMetadata(epicID, map[string]interface{}{
      "mindspec_phase": derived, "mindspec_migrated_at":
      time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)})` call. Return
      `(true, nil)` on success, or `(false, err)` on write
      failure.
   5. The helper is idempotent: calling it twice on the same
      already-migrated epic returns `(false, nil)` the second
      time (because step 2's stored-phase read short-circuits).
9. **First-command auto-migration call sites.** The following
   three entry points each call `phase.EnsureMigrated(specID)` at
   the top of their flow, before any phase-dependent logic:
   - `internal/approve/plan.go::PlanApprove(specID, ...)` —
     immediately after spec ID validation, before the existing
     plan-validation steps.
   - `internal/approve/impl.go::ImplApprove(specID, ...)` —
     immediately after spec ID validation, before the existing
     impl-validation steps.
   - `internal/complete/complete.go::Complete(...)` — immediately
     after spec ID resolution, before the existing
     `phase.DerivePhaseFromChildren(children)` call at line 404.
   Errors from `EnsureMigrated` are surfaced to the caller (the
   command fails; the operator sees the error). A successful
   migration emits a structured log line to stderr in the existing
   `internal/log` format (`event=lifecycle.migrated spec=<id>
   epic=<id> phase=<derived>`).
10. **`approve spec` does NOT call `EnsureMigrated`.** `approve
    spec` is the *creator* of the lifecycle epic; it cannot
    migrate an epic that does not yet exist. The auto-migration is
    a no-op when called on a newly-created epic (because the new
    epic has `mindspec_phase: plan` from creation per spec 080), so
    no call is needed and no test regression occurs. `approve
    spec`'s only behaviour change in this spec is the addition of
    the `TestApproveSpecCreatesSingleBead` assertion confirming
    that exactly one bead exists for the spec after the call.
11. **`mindspec doctor --dry-run-migration` flag.** New boolean
    flag on `mindspec doctor`. When set, the doctor:
    - Walks `.mindspec/docs/specs/*/` (top-level entries only).
    - For each spec ID found, calls `phase.FindEpicBySpecID(specID)`.
    - For each epic, checks for `mindspec_phase` in metadata.
    - For each epic *lacking* `mindspec_phase`, reads children via
      `queryChildren` (no cache needed here; doctor is cold), calls
      `DerivePhaseFromChildren(children)` to compute the derived
      phase, and prints one line per legacy spec:
      `would-migrate: spec=<id> epic=<id> phase=<derived>`.
    - Writes NOTHING. The metadata is not touched; no
      `mindspec_migrated_at` is stamped. The flag is a pure
      reporter.
    - Exit code is `0` if all walks succeed (regardless of how
      many legacy specs exist), non-zero only on walk error.
12. **`mindspec_migrated_at` metadata field.** RFC3339 UTC
    timestamp written by `EnsureMigrated` alongside
    `mindspec_phase`. The field's presence is the durable marker
    that a migration ran; its absence (combined with the presence
    of `mindspec_phase`) indicates a post-080 native epic.
    Operators can grep epic metadata for `mindspec_migrated_at` to
    list specs that were migrated from the legacy 7-bead layout.
13. **Ceremony children are NOT deleted.** Legacy 7-bead specs
    retain their child beads after migration. Post-migration, the
    children are ignored by `DerivePhase` (because
    `readStoredPhaseWithCache` short-circuits with the now-present
    `mindspec_phase`). Operators may close, ignore, or manually
    delete the children at their discretion; mindspec does not
    touch them. This preserves the back-compat property in the
    plan's risk note: "Legacy specs that operators do not touch
    never trigger migration. Acceptable — they continue working
    via the metadata-or-children-derived dual read."
14. **`DerivePhaseFromChildren` fallback retained.** The
    children-derived fallback at `internal/phase/derive.go:141`
    and `:144` is preserved as the last-resort back-stop for
    legacy epics that have never had a lifecycle command run
    against them (so `EnsureMigrated` has never fired). Removal of
    this fallback is a future-major-version concern, explicitly
    out of scope (see Scope: Out).
15. **ADR-0034 drafted with `Domain(s): workflow`.** ADR-0034
    must list `workflow` in its `Domain(s)` field so the spec 087
    plan-time cite-relevant gate passes against this spec's single
    impacted domain. The ADR records the decisions listed in the
    `## ADR Touchpoints` block above.

## ADR Decisions

### ADR-0034

Three sub-decisions:

1. **Auto-migrate on first lifecycle command (no opt-in).** On entry
   to `approve plan`, `approve impl`, or `complete`: if the target
   spec's lifecycle epic lacks the `mindspec_phase` metadata key,
   derive the phase from existing ceremony children once, write
   `mindspec_phase` plus `mindspec_migrated_at` to the epic, then
   proceed with the original command. The migration is transparent
   and idempotent — second invocations see the metadata and skip the
   derive step. Rejected alternatives: require an explicit
   `--migrate` flag (operator friction, indefinite drift in practice);
   leave legacy specs alone forever (the children-derivation fallback
   stays load-bearing and the metadata-first invariant never becomes
   true across the fleet).

2. **Ceremony children kept, ignored after migration.** The migration
   does not delete the legacy ceremony beads — it only writes the
   metadata. The children-derived fallback in `phase.DerivePhase`
   remains in the binary so that legacy specs which never trigger a
   lifecycle command (archived, abandoned, read-only history) still
   resolve to a sensible phase. Rejected: delete ceremony children on
   migration (destructive, irreversible, blocks any historical audit
   that walks children); delete the children-derived fallback in
   `DerivePhase` (breaks every unmigrated spec the instant the binary
   ships).

3. **`mindspec doctor --dry-run-migration`** reports every spec that
   would migrate on its next lifecycle command, showing the derived
   phase value and the source children, without writing any state.
   Operators get visibility into the upcoming migration surface
   before any production lifecycle command runs.

### ADR-0030

Three sub-decisions:

1. **Boundary scope: git AND process I/O via Executor; bd via
   `internal/bead`.** Enforcement packages may not import `os/exec` or
   `internal/gitutil`. They route git through Executor and bd through
   `internal/bead` (which itself may shell out, but acts as the single
   point of mediation per the transformation plan's "bd stays wrapped"
   stance). Rejected alternatives: routing bd through Executor (rejected
   per plan — would expand Executor surface unnecessarily); allowing
   direct `exec.Command` for "trivial" cases (rejected — defeats the
   lint).

2. **AST-based boundary lint at `internal/lint/boundary_test.go`.**
   Primary gate: `os/exec` / `internal/gitutil` import bans on
   enforcement packages. Secondary gate: literal-walker that catches
   `exec.Command("git", …)` / `exec.Command("bd", …)` style call sites
   (with constant-folding to catch `const cmd = "git"` cases). Rejected:
   grep-based check (per plan: "AST-based, not grep").

3. **Opt-in escape hatch: leading comment
   `// boundary-allowlisted: <reason and reviewer-id>`.** The lint
   recognizes the marker and exempts the file; reviewer-gated, not
   default. Rejected: no escape hatch at all (too rigid for legitimate
   future needs).

### ADR-0032

Four sub-decisions:

1. **Domain identifier is the `OWNERSHIP.yaml` directory name.** All
   three artifacts (spec.md `## Impacted Domains`, `OWNERSHIP.yaml`
   location, ADR `Domains` field) MUST use the same short-tag identifier
   set (e.g., `core`, `execution`). Comparison is case-folded,
   trim-whitespace, exact set intersection. No aliases or hierarchy in
   v1. Rejected alternatives: path-like identifiers (ambiguous —
   `internal/foo` vs `foo`); free-form tags (impossible to validate
   mechanically).

2. **Plan-time gate: cite-relevant + coverage-complete.** Extends
   `checkADRCitations` to intersect `ADR.Domains` with the spec's
   impacted-domains — empty intersection is an error. A new
   `checkADRCoverage` ensures every impacted domain has at least one
   cited Accepted ADR whose `Domains` contains it. Rejected: cite-
   relevant only (allows uncovered domains to slip through); a separate
   `mindspec adr verify` step (defers the check past plan approval where
   it belongs).

3. **Bead-time gate: divergence check via `Executor.ChangedFiles` +
   `attributeDomain`.** `internal/validate/divergence.go::ValidateDivergence`
   computes the diff range, maps paths to domains via the F2
   `OWNERSHIP.yaml` machinery, and errors when a touched domain isn't in
   the plan's cited ADR coverage. The `internal/validate/adr_divergence.go`
   stub from spec 086 calls into this. `approve impl` runs the same
   check as a backstop with broader scope (main → spec branch).

4. **Override flags with split audit trail.** `--override-adr "<reason>"`
   records `mindspec_adr_override_*` keys in bead metadata (one-shot
   pass-through, reason required). `--supersede-adr ADR-NNNN` is a
   richer form: it creates a new ADR with `Status: Proposed` and
   `Domains` seeded from the violated domain, AND records
   `mindspec_adr_supersede_*` metadata, AND bypasses the gate (the gate
   is not re-run since the new ADR is `Proposed` not `Accepted`; full
   upgrade to `Accepted` is a follow-up). No env-var escape hatch.
   Metadata writes happen AFTER terminal mutation success, consistent
   with ADR-0031 discipline.

title: [089-ceremony-collapse] Bead 2 — `phase.EnsureMigrated` helper + three call sites
description:
**Title**: `[089] phase.EnsureMigrated auto-migrator + approve/complete call sites`
**Type**: `task`
**Parent epic**: spec 089 lifecycle epic
**Depends on**: Bead 1 (so the scaffolding regression test is in
place before any phase-package code lands)
**Estimated effort**: ~3-4 h

### Goal

Add the exported `phase.EnsureMigrated(specID string) (migrated
bool, err error)` helper to `internal/phase/derive.go` and wire
it into `approve.PlanApprove`, `approve.ImplApprove`, and
`complete.Complete` as the first phase-touching call in each
flow.

### Files touched

- **MODIFIED** `internal/phase/derive.go` — append the new
  `EnsureMigrated` function (and a `mergeMetadataFn` indirection
  + `SetMergeMetadataForTest` helper for the test seam) after
  the existing `DerivePhaseFromChildren` block (around line
  222). Do NOT modify any existing exported surface.
- **MODIFIED** `internal/approve/plan.go` — insert
  `phase.EnsureMigrated(specID)` call immediately after spec ID
  validation, before existing plan-validation logic.
- **MODIFIED** `internal/approve/impl.go` — insert
  `phase.EnsureMigrated(specID)` call immediately after spec ID
  validation, before existing impl-validation logic.
- **MODIFIED** `internal/complete/complete.go` — insert
  `phase.EnsureMigrated(specID)` call immediately after spec ID
  resolution, before the existing
  `phase.DerivePhaseFromChildren` call at line 404.
- **NEW** `internal/phase/migrate_test.go` — the new helper's
  unit tests.
- **MODIFIED** `internal/approve/plan_test.go`,
  `internal/approve/impl_test.go`,
  `internal/complete/complete_test.go` — add the three
  `TestXCallsEnsureMigrated` extensions using existing seams
  (`SetPlanMergeMetadataForTest` at `plan.go:39`,
  `implMergeMetadataFn` at `impl.go:26`, plus the new
  `phase.SetMergeMetadataForTest`).

**Steps**

1. **Helper definition.** In `internal/phase/derive.go`, add a
   package-level `mergeMetadataFn = bead.MergeMetadata`
   indirection + `SetMergeMetadataForTest` helper following the
   existing `SetRunBDForTest` pattern at line 27. Append at
   the end of file (after the existing `DerivePhaseFromChildren`
   function around line 222):

   ```go
   // EnsureMigrated runs one-shot legacy-to-metadata migration for
   // the spec's lifecycle epic. Returns (migrated bool, err error).
   //
   // Per ADR-0034: if the spec's epic lacks mindspec_phase metadata
   // (legacy pre-080 7-bead layout), derive the phase from existing
   // ceremony children once, write mindspec_phase + mindspec_migrated_at,
   // and return (true, nil). If the metadata is already present, or no
   // epic exists yet (pre-approve-spec), return (false, nil).
   //
   // Idempotent: a second call on a migrated epic returns (false, nil).
   func EnsureMigrated(specID string) (bool, error) {
       epicID, err := FindEpicBySpecID(specID)
       if err != nil || epicID == "" {
           return false, nil // nothing to migrate
       }
       c := NewCache()
       if storedPhase := readStoredPhaseWithCache(c, epicID); storedPhase != "" {
           return false, nil // already migrated or post-080 native
       }
       children, _ := c.GetChildren(epicID)
       derived := DerivePhaseFromChildren(children)
       if err := mergeMetadataFn(epicID, map[string]interface{}{
           "mindspec_phase":       derived,
           "mindspec_migrated_at": time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
       }); err != nil {
           return false, fmt.Errorf("ensure-migrated %s: %w", specID, err)
       }
       fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "event=lifecycle.migrated spec=%s epic=%s phase=%s\n",
           specID, epicID, derived)
       return true, nil
   }
   ```

   Add `"time"` to the existing import block. The `bead.MergeMetadata`
   reference uses the existing `internal/bead` import on line 11.

2. **Call site — `internal/approve/plan.go::PlanApprove`.**
   After the existing `validate.SpecID(specID)` check near the
   top of `PlanApprove`, insert:

   ```go
   if _, err := phase.EnsureMigrated(specID); err != nil {
       return nil, err
   }
   ```

   The error is surfaced to the caller — a migration write
   failure fails the command, per spec Requirement 9.

3. **Call site — `internal/approve/impl.go::ImplApprove`.** Same
   insertion immediately after `validate.SpecID(specID)` near
   the top of `ImplApprove`.

4. **Call site — `internal/complete/complete.go::Complete`.**
   Insert immediately after the spec ID resolution block (in
   `Complete` itself, before any call that reaches
   `advanceState(root, specID)` at line 393 → which calls
   `phase.DerivePhaseFromChildren` at line 404). The call must
   precede any phase-dependent read.

5. **Tests in `internal/phase/migrate_test.go`.** Use the new
   `phase.SetMergeMetadataForTest` seam plus the existing
   `phase.SetRunBDForTest` / `phase.SetListJSONForTest` seams to
   stub bd. The four `TestEnsureMigrated*` tests in the Testing
   Strategy block above all sit in this file.

6. **Tests in approve/complete packages.** Each test:
   1. Stubs `FindEpicBySpecID` (via the bd seam) to return a
      legacy epic ID, stubs the child query to return the
      7-bead layout, stubs the relevant `MergeMetadata` seam.
   2. Calls the approve/complete entry point.
   3. Asserts the captured `MergeMetadata` invocations show
      `mindspec_phase` + `mindspec_migrated_at` written BEFORE
      any plan/impl/complete-specific bd call.

**Verification**

- [ ] `go build ./... && go test -short ./...` is green.
- [ ] `go test -run "Migrate|Migrated" ./internal/phase/...
  ./internal/approve/... ./internal/complete/...` is green.
- [ ] Existing `TestDerivePhaseFromChildren` passes unchanged.
- [ ] AST boundary lint (`internal/lint/boundary_test.go`)
  stays green — the helper adds no `os/exec` or git imports;
  it uses only `bead.MergeMetadata` + the existing `phase`
  package internals. Per HC-6.

**Acceptance Criteria**

- [ ] `TestPhaseDerivationFromMetadataOnly` passes; verifies
  `DerivePhaseWithStatusWithCache` short-circuits on stored
  `mindspec_phase` without calling `queryChildren`.
- [ ] `TestLegacyMigratesOnFirstCommand` proven via
  `TestPlanApproveCallsEnsureMigrated`,
  `TestImplApproveCallsEnsureMigrated`, and
  `TestCompleteCallsEnsureMigrated` — each asserts the
  `MergeMetadata` write of `mindspec_phase` +
  `mindspec_migrated_at` happens before any phase-dependent
  bd call.
- [ ] `TestMigratedEpicHasMigratedAtMarker` passes
  (`TestEnsureMigratedLegacyEpicWritesPhaseAndTimestamp`
  asserts the RFC3339 UTC timestamp is parseable).
- [ ] `TestEnsureMigratedIdempotent` passes; second call on a
  migrated fixture returns `(false, nil)` with NO further
  `MergeMetadata` invocation.
- [ ] `TestEnsureMigratedNoEpicReturnsFalseNil` passes; when
  `FindEpicBySpecID` returns `""` the helper returns
  `(false, nil)` with NO bd write.
- [ ] `TestDerivePhaseFromChildrenStillPasses`: the existing
  `internal/phase/derive_test.go::TestDerivePhaseFromChildren`
  runs unchanged (preservation enforced by Bead 2 not touching
  `DerivePhaseFromChildren`'s body, per spec Requirement 14).
- [ ] `TestCompleteUnchangedOnPostMigrationFixture`: existing
  `internal/complete/complete_test.go` fixtures continue to
  pass because `EnsureMigrated` writes the children-derived
  value by construction.
- [ ] `go build ./... && go test -short ./...` exits 0 per
  HC-5; AST boundary lint stays green per HC-6.

### Commit message

```
[089] add phase.EnsureMigrated auto-migrator + wire approve/complete

Introduces phase.EnsureMigrated(specID) which converts legacy 7-bead
specs to the metadata-first representation on first lifecycle command:
derives mindspec_phase from existing children once, writes it plus
mindspec_migrated_at (RFC3339 UTC), then returns. Idempotent.

Wires three call sites: approve.PlanApprove, approve.ImplApprove,
complete.Complete. Each calls EnsureMigrated immediately after spec ID
resolution; migration errors fail the command.

Per ADR-0034 and spec 089 Requirements 8, 9, 12.
AC: TestLegacyMigratesOnFirstCommand, TestMigratedEpicHasMigratedAtMarker,
TestEnsureMigratedIdempotent, TestEnsureMigratedNoEpicReturnsFalseNil.
```
